name: Sylius Open Source PHP E-Commerce Framework API
what_it_does: Open-source PHP e-commerce framework built on Symfony with API-first REST architecture — enabling development teams to build highly customized B2B and B2C e-commerce solutions using API Platform, with product management, order processing, customer management, promotions, and headless delivery via REST API, designed as a flexible foundation for custom e-commerce builds.
use_cases: Building customized B2B e-commerce systems using Sylius REST API as headless backend
Managing product catalog, pricing rules, and promotions via Sylius API for catalog automation
Processing orders and managing fulfillment via Sylius Order API for ERP integration
Building multi-channel e-commerce (web, mobile, marketplace) with Sylius as headless API backend
Extending Sylius with custom business logic using Symfony bundle system for specialized e-commerce
not_for: Non-PHP/Symfony organizations (Sylius requires deep Symfony expertise for customization)
Out-of-the-box e-commerce without development (Sylius is a framework, not a turnkey solution)
Teams preferring SaaS over self-hosted (Sylius is self-hosted only)
best_when: A PHP/Symfony development team needs maximum e-commerce customization flexibility for a complex B2B platform — where Magento is too heavy, Shopify is too restrictive, and a purpose-built Symfony API-first framework enables unique business logic.
avoid_when: You don't have Symfony expertise, need out-of-the-box features without development, prefer SaaS e-commerce, or are building simple consumer retail.

auth.notes: OAuth2 via LeagueOAuth2 bundle for user-delegated access. API token for system integrations. Bearer token auth. Channel-scoped access (Sylius multi-channel). No core webhooks — Symfony EventDispatcher for custom events. REST API built on API Platform (JSON:API and JSON-LD). Open source (MIT license) — full source code. Self-hosted Symfony application. No fine-grained scopes beyond role hierarchy.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source MIT license — free forever for self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: Sylius Plus (B2B features) from €5,000/year; Enterprise support custom; contact sales
pricing.notes: Polish company (Wrocław). Founded 2011 by Paweł Jędrzejewski. Open source (MIT). GitHub 7,000+ stars. Symfony ecosystem-native. Sylius Plus for B2B features (multi-organization, returns, wishlist, channel pricing). 1,000+ production stores. Used by PepsiCo, Dell, Akeneo. Strong in European B2B commerce. API Platform-based REST API with OpenAPI spec generation. DDD/hexagonal architecture for maintainability. Akeneo (PIM) deep integration. Strong developer community.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API via API Platform (JSON:API + JSON-LD). OAuth2 + token auth. Good developer documentation. OpenAPI spec auto-generated. No core webhooks — implement via Symfony Events. PHP/Symfony developer required. Self-hosted. Polish company with GDPR compliance. API Platform provides rich query capabilities (filtering, sorting, pagination).
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product reads fully idempotent. Order reads idempotent by order token. Customer reads idempotent by email. API Platform pagination is cursor-like using page number.
known_agent_gotchas: PHP/Symfony developer required — deep Symfony knowledge needed for customization
API Platform generates API from entities — may have complex JSON-LD response format
No core webhooks — implement as Symfony Event Listeners for production event-driven integration
B2B features (Sylius Plus) require paid license — free tier is B2C focused
Multi-channel architecture adds complexity — channel context required for most API calls

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced (deployment-controlled). GDPR compliant (operator responsibility). Self-hosted — full data residency control. OAuth2 via LeagueOAuth2. Open source MIT allows full security audit. Symfony security patches via Composer. PCI-DSS for payment handling — use payment gateway (Stripe, Braintree) for card data. Polish company — strong GDPR awareness. Sylius security team for vulnerability reporting. Self-hosted gives maximum control over security posture.
